# [clang] Assertion `AddressOfExpr->getType() == Context.OverloadTy' failed.

**Author:** yijan4845
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/115607

## Body

**This testcase is generated by a fuzzer.**

Compiler Explorer: [https://godbolt.org/z/fvWKKccTs](https://godbolt.org/z/fvWKKccTs)

This invalid code will crash on clang trunk and clang assertion trunk:
```cpp
namespace std {

  template<typename T> struct remove_reference { using type = T; };
  template<typename T> struct remove_reference<T&> { using type = T; };
  template<typename T> struct remove_reference<T&&> { using type = T; };

  template<typename T> struct is_lvalue_reference { static constexpr bool value = false; const _Bool v1 = false;; };

  template <class T> struct is_const { static constexpr bool value = false; const _Bool v2 = false; };
  template <class T> struct is_const<const T> { static constexpr bool value = true; };

  template <bool B, class T, class F> struct conditional { using type = T; };
  template <class T, class F> struct conditional<false, T, F> { using type = F; };

  template <class U, class T>
  using CopyConst = typename conditional<is_const<remove_reference<U>>::value, const T, T>::type;

  template <class U, class T>
  using OverrideRef = typename conditional<
          is_lvalue_reference<U &&>::value,
          typename remove_reference<T>::type &,
          typename remove_reference<T>::type &&>::type;

  template <class U, class T>
  using ForwardLikeRetType = OverrideRef<U &&, CopyConst<U, T>>;

  template <class U, class T>
  constexpr auto forward_like(T &&t) -> ForwardLikeRetType<U, T> {
    using TT = typename remove_reference<T>::type;
    static_assert(TT::moveable, "instantiated as_const"); 
  }
}
struct B {};
B &&(*pForwardLike)(B&) = &std::forward_like<int&&, B&>;
```

It seems that this goes back to clang-19.

Stack dump:
```
clang++: /root/llvm-project/clang/lib/Sema/SemaOverload.cpp:13264: clang::FunctionDecl* clang::Sema::ResolveAddressOfOverloadedFunction(clang::Expr*, clang::QualType, bool, clang::DeclAccessPair&, bool*): Assertion `AddressOfExpr->getType() == Context.OverloadTy' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.
Stack dump:
```

This invalid code will crash on clang trunk and clang assertion trunk:
```cpp
namespace std {

  template&lt;typename T&gt; struct remove_reference { using type = T; };
  template&lt;typename T&gt; struct remove_reference&lt;T&amp;&gt; { using type = T; };
  template&lt;typename T&gt; struct remove_reference&lt;T&amp;&amp;&gt; { using type = T; };

  template&lt;typename T&gt; struct is_lvalue_reference { static constexpr bool value = false; const _Bool v1 = false;; };

  template &lt;class T&gt; struct is_const { static constexpr bool value = false; const _Bool v2 = false; };
  template &lt;class T&gt; struct is_const&lt;const T&gt; { static constexpr bool value = true; };

  template &lt;bool B, class T, class F&gt; struct conditional { using type = T; };
  template &lt;class T, class F&gt; struct conditional&lt;false, T, F&gt; { using type = F; };

  template &lt;class U, class T&gt;
  using CopyConst = typename conditional&lt;is_const&lt;remove_reference&lt;U&gt;&gt;::value, const T, T&gt;::type;

  template &lt;class U, class T&gt;
  using OverrideRef = typename conditional&lt;
          is_lvalue_reference&lt;U &amp;&amp;&gt;::value,
          typename remove_reference&lt;T&gt;::type &amp;,
          typename remove_reference&lt;T&gt;::type &amp;&amp;&gt;::type;

  template &lt;class U, class T&gt;
  using ForwardLikeRetType = OverrideRef&lt;U &amp;&amp;, CopyConst&lt;U, T&gt;&gt;;

  template &lt;class U, class T&gt;
  constexpr auto forward_like(T &amp;&amp;t) -&gt; ForwardLikeRetType&lt;U, T&gt; {
    using TT = typename remove_reference&lt;T&gt;::type;
    static_assert(TT::moveable, "instantiated as_const"); 
  }
}
struct B {};
B &amp;&amp;(*pForwardLike)(B&amp;) = &amp;std::forward_like&lt;int&amp;&amp;, B&amp;&gt;;
```

---

### Comment 2 - shafik

@sdkrystian git bisect points to this commit as the cause of the regression: 77c5cea78eac3f20d0ba79f5892235e5aac82603

---

### Comment 3 - ojhunt

Minimized test case:
```cpp
template <class T> struct S1 { static constexpr bool value = false; const X b = false; };
template <class T> struct S2 { static constexpr bool value = false; const X b = false; };
template <bool b1, bool b2, class T> using Test = T;

template <class T>
constexpr Test<S1<T>::value, S2<T>::value, T> forward_like(T &t) {
    return T();
}


int (*pForwardLike)(int&) = &forward_like<int>;
```

The instantiation of Test<> requires S1 and S2 be different, it requires all three parameters, etc

If you change any of these parts we don't trigger the assertion and get expected behavior.

---

### Comment 4 - ojhunt

Ok the _regression_ was introduced by the logic at https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/07da480614cd7ce3f105a153626a23f6888e503f/clang/lib/Sema/SemaInit.cpp#L8870

```cpp
    if (OnlyArg->getType() == S.Context.OverloadTy) {
      DeclAccessPair Found;
      if (FunctionDecl *FD = S.ResolveAddressOfOverloadedFunction(
              OnlyArg, DestType.getNonReferenceType(), /*Complain=*/false,
              Found)) {
        if (Expr *Resolved =
                S.FixOverloadedFunctionReference(OnlyArg, Found, FD).get())
          OnlyArg = Resolved;
      }
    }
```

If this path is taken after we have recorded a `FK_AddressOfOverloadFailed` failure we try to diagnose the error assuming that OnlyArg is still pointing to the original failed overload expression. That is the cause for the assert. We can easily resolve this assert by simply recording the original expression and passing that or making sure we have still have an overload failure in the FK_AddressOfOverloadFailed case.

While those will resolve the assertion I don't think that's the actual bug.

In the test case above we can change the `forward_like` decl from
```cpp
template <class T>
constexpr Test<S1<T>::value, S2<T>::value, T> forward_like(T &t) 
```

to

```cpp
template <class T>
constexpr Test<S1<T>::value, S1<T>::value, T> forward_like(T &t) 
```

and the assertion stops happening. These should have identical instantiation behavior, so there's no reason that the diagnostic path should be different: either both versions should be triggering this assertion, or neither should. I think the fact that one does and on does not implies something incorrect is happening in the template resolution or instantiation paths.

---
